description: Touched down in Bali and hopped on a Grab taxi for the scenic 1.5-hour drive through lush rice fields and jungle roads to our villa in Ubud.
Checked into a gorgeous private pool villa in the heart of Ubud — surrounded by tropical gardens with the sound of frogs and distant temple bells.
Rented scooters and rode through Ubud's winding lanes — the best way to navigate the town and discover hidden temples around every corner.
Wandered through the famous Pasar Seni market as dusk settled — rows of batik sarongs
Strolled around the ornate Puri Saren Agung Palace — the spiritual and cultural heart of Ubud
First dinner in Bali at This Is Bali — authentic Balinese cuisine in a lush garden courtyard. The perfect welcome to the island.
Thrilling ATV ride through jungle trails
cost_currency: and river valleys — an adrenaline-packed morning exploring terrain that scooters can't reach.

description: White-water rafting through the Ayung River gorge — 2.5km of rapids past ancient stone carvings and lush canyon walls. Lunch included at the end.
Afternoon recovery in the villa — a long swim in the private pool and a proper rest before the evening plans.
Spectacular fine dining at Taman Dedari — a theatrical dinner among lily ponds and Balinese sculptures with traditional dance performances in the background.
A serene lake temple floating on morning mist over Lake Beratan — one of Bali's most photogenic and spiritually significant temples.
The most beautiful waterfall in Bali — a 45-minute jungle trek leads to a stunning curtain of water plunging 80m into a moss-covered gorge.
Continued the waterfall trail to the lesser-visited Hidden and Fiji falls — pristine natural pools and towering jungle canopy.
Lunch at the incredible cliff-edge Hanging Cafe overlooking the valley — Balinese set menu and the best view you can possibly eat lunch with.
Relaxed evening in the private pool villa after a big day of waterfalls — villa pool and homemade dinner from supermarket groceries.
A 2am start for the legendary Mt Batur sunrise hike. Scrambled up the volcanic trail by torchlight and reached the crater rim just as the sky turned gold.
Returned from the summit and crashed for a well-earned sleep — tired legs but a heart full of one of Bali's greatest experiences.
A unique Balinese attraction with floating bamboo platforms over a spring pool — traditional food served in a serene garden landscape.
Final evening in Ubud exploring the market and surrounding streets — stocked up on snacks and browsed local crafts before tomorrow's ferry.

place_name: Sanur Harbour - Ferry to Nusa Penida
description: Caught the fast boat from Sanur to Nusa Penida with premium seats on the upper deck — 45 minutes across the Bali Strait.
Settled into Nusa Penida and headed straight to La Coco for a fresh coconut and grilled fish lunch with views of the jungle-lined coast.
One of the most dramatic beaches in Southeast Asia — pristine white sand at the base of a towering cliff
cost_amount: reached via steep wooden stairs. Absolutely jaw-dropping.

description: A hidden crescent of white sand flanked by dramatic limestone pillars rising from the turquoise sea. Almost too perfect to be real.
Golden hour at the famous Thousand Island viewpoint — tiny islets scattered across the glittering sea below
cost_amount: with Roman Pohan temple ruins on the clifftop.

description: Dinner at an open-air restaurant overlooking the ocean — grilled prawns and fresh fish as waves crashed on the rocks below.
The unmistakable T-Rex shaped cliff of Nusa Penida — arguably the most photographed spot in all of Indonesia and every bit as stunning in person.
A collapsed sea cave forming a dramatic natural circular cove — vivid aquamarine water swirling inside the ancient rock arch. Mesmerising.
The famous natural infinity pool carved into the coastal rock — turquoise water meeting the open ocean. Visited at low tide for the best experience.
An afternoon dip at Crystal Bay — one of Nusa Penida's clearest beaches with calm water perfect for snorkelling. Spotted a sea turtle.
Dinner on the terrace at Mambo Beach Resort — watching the last light fade over the bay as the perfect finale to an incredible West Nusa Penida day.
Caught the morning fast boat back to Bali with premium seats — waved goodbye to Nusa Penida through the sea spray as the island disappeared behind us.
Checked into the Seminyak apartment for the final stretch of the trip — well-located for the beach strip and the south's big-ticket sights.
A decidedly un-authentic but wholly satisfying McDonalds lunch — sometimes you just need a burger after a week of temples and volcanos.
Late afternoon stroll along the famous Seminyak beach stretch — watching surfers carve through the breaks and local vendors hawk cold drinks.
Explore Seminyak Village — a stylish open-air mall with boutique stores
Walk along Jalan Raya Seminyak — lined with trendy boutiques
Continued north to Canggu's Echo Beach at golden hour — a more local crowd
Drove south to the stunning Melasti — one of Bali's most pristine white sand beaches tucked below towering limestone cliffs.
The famous surf beach from Eat Pray Love — entered through a cave passage carved into the cliff to reach the photogenic cove below.
Lunch at the legendary Single Fin — a cliff-top restaurant and bar directly above the Uluwatu surf break. Perfect food and an unbeatable view.
Pura Luhur Uluwatu perched on a 70-metre sea cliff — one of Bali's six key temples
cost_amount: with sweeping Indian Ocean views and resident monkeys.

description: The hypnotic sunset Kecak dance at Uluwatu — 150 men chanting in unison around a fire as the sky blazed behind the cliff temple. Spine-tingling.
Ended the big day with a quick KFC dinner before heading into Bali Galleria for a satisfying Uniqlo shopping haul.
A late start with a visit to the upscale Beach Walk mall on the Kuta beachfront — air-conditioned browsing and souvenir hunting.
Chicken teriyaki lunch at a restaurant in the mall — a satisfying and wallet-friendly option before the afternoon treat.
A 90-minute traditional Balinese aroma massage at Fajar Bali Spa — the most restorative experience of the trip. Not optional. Highly recommended.
Final round of souvenir shopping at Level 21 — gifts for home and a Miniso haul to round out the trip.
A Bali institution and the perfect final dinner — legendary wood-fired ribs with Naughty Nuri's famous sauce and cold Bintangs. Non-negotiable.
Ended the last full night in Bali the right way — live music and Bintangs at BaliJo until the early hours.
One last Bali morning — coffee and a snack from a local warung while watching the street wake up outside the Seminyak flat.
Grabbed a Grab taxi to the airport for the last time. Said goodbye to Bali through the window on the way out.
Transit lunch at Singapore Changi — a 7-Eleven stop between flights. Bali in the rear-view and a full camera roll of memories.
